entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Putin to visit Tehran; U.S. says Iran plans to send Russia drones
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe at slain former leader’s funeral
  • Mexican president meets Biden amid tensions over migration and fentanyl
  • Brother of disgraced Sri Lankan president blocked from fleeing country
  • U.S. to open new embassies, boost aid in Pacific as China’s sway grows
  • Recording reveals life in captivity for American held by Russian group
  • Fake cricket league hired farmers as pros, duped Russian gamblers, police say
  • French Parliament to debate Macron’s Uber dealings, as inquiry looms
  • Within the war between Russia and Ukraine, a war between Chechens
  • Uber Files put Macron’s neoliberalism in the spotlight
  • Iran to send hundreds of drones to Russia for use in Ukraine, U.S. says
  • White House says Iran may provide Russia with hundreds of drones
  • Russian strikes kill at least three in Kharkiv and injure dozens
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Macron’s Uber dealings may prompt parliamentary inquiry in France
  • World population to reach 8 billion by November even as growth slows
  • Uber wooed Russia’s rich and powerful but failed there anyway
  • As Uber steamrolled into France, Emmanuel Macron was a ‘true ally’
  • Europe braces for gas ‘nightmare’ as pipeline from Russia shuts off
  • Prosecutors investigate explosives placed in central Warsaw
  • Ukraine reports striking Russian ammunition depot in south
  • Lawyers: Man convicted in 2015 Paris attacks won't appeal
  • Putin set to visit Iran next week
  • Iran arrests 3rd outspoken filmmaker in escalating crackdown
  • It’s been a hard time for librarians. Enter an epic sleepover of stuffed toys.
  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Pa. town broke law in recent hiring of Tamir Rice’s killer, A.G. says
  • Gerald McEntee, longtime president of AFSCME labor union, dies at 87
  • Post Politics Now: ‘It matters, but it’s not enough,’ Biden says of new gun law
  • Jan. 6 hearing expected to focus on link between militants, White House
  • Parkland dad disrupts White House event on gun control
  • Judge rejects Bannon’s bid to delay trial, executive-privilege claim
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • At Invitation Homes, unpermitted work leaves leaky plumbing, faulty repairs, renters say
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • Is Amazon Prime worth it for you?
  • In leaked memo, Facebook tells managers low performers don’t belong
  • You can build your own wood fence and get fancy with it
  • First residents move into former Fannie Mae headquarters
  • What’s behind rising rents and what can be done?
  • Drought for Rainmakers Threatens Job Cuts for Bankers
  • Benettons Look Past Travel Chaos in Dufry Deal
  • Unicorn Stock Markets: A Lifeboat for Startup Employees?
  • Successor Beware! Boris Johnson Has a Real Legacy.
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why Japan Will Struggle to Do Without Russian Energy
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • Why the Euro Has Tumbled Near Parity to the US Dollar
  • How Cuba’s investment in writers and artists came back to haunt its regime
  • If pols can’t make inflation better, they should at least stop making it worse
  • Republicans won’t say what they’re for. But it’s not hard to figure out.
  • The Biden administration’s bold embrace of harm reduction will save lives
  • Vanita Gupta on the future of policing and American democracy
  • Shorten the war. Send 60 HIMARS to Ukraine.
  • As Latino voters shift right, here’s where Democrats will be hit hardest
  • We should worry what happens if Trump never faces justice, not if he does
  • Conservatives hope to turn back the cultural clock. Can they succeed?
  • Biden’s scheme to cap oil prices reflects his incoherent Russia policy
  • Democrats are overreacting about the Supreme Court
  • Biden needs to address the coming crisis between Israel and Palestine
  • Middle East policy is too important to be guided by short-term concerns
  • Confirm Hanes to the Eastern District
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:

entry.content:

  • Latvia president calls for defence spending boost, compulsory military service for all
  • Sanctions-hit firms may get 500,000 euros state aid under EU plans
  • Death toll from Russian rocket attack on Ukraine housing block rises to 30
  • UK's Johnson steers clear of endorsing successor
  • No fries till autumn at some of McDonald's Russian successor restaurants
CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-12 10:50AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Major study into effects of long Covid to be carried out by Chinese University of Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teens acquitted of manslaughter for roles in janitor’s death, but found guilty of rioting
  • Hong Kong public transport firms Citybus and New World First Bus to join forces in bid to stem losses
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong public hospitals suspend some elective surgical operations as city logs over 2,700 infections
  • Hong Kong man, 26, falls to death from flat after ‘leaning out of window to retrieve wireless earphone’
  • Hong Kong veteran activist Koo Sze-yiu sentenced to 9 months in jail for planning to display coffin bearing slogans outside Beijing’s liaison office
  • Proposed Covid-19 health code for Hong Kong will not be used for any political agenda, ‘only pandemic purposes’: John Lee
  • What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new health code system to identify coronavirus cases
  • Hong Kong’s nano flats: does falling demand point to the end of container-size abodes?
  • Hong Kong’s use of electronic tracking wristbands not ‘foolproof’ solution to deter Covid-19 patients from breaching quarantine, experts say
  • Hong Kong should offer free breast cancer screening as a life-saving priority
  • Beijing lambasts top US diplomat over ‘let Hong Kong be Hong Kong’ speech, accusing him of ‘grandstanding and hypocrisy’
  • Hong Kong considers tougher penalties for residents caught feeding wild pigeons
  • Coronavirus: Care homes in Hong Kong struggle to find staff as they brace for possible sixth wave of infections
  • Macau’s citywide coronavirus lockdown confuses and frustrates residents
  • Hong Kong sweats on hottest day of year so far as temperature hits 37.4 degrees Celsius in some parts of city
  • China’s latest wearable tech is an air-conditioning system tailored for hazmat suits
  • China ‘not to blame’ for African debt crisis, it’s the West: study
  • Should Hong Kong give the red light to mainland-style coronavirus health codes?
  • No summer treat for China’s young gamers: Tencent says three hours a week game time limit remains
  • China’s banking-crisis victims promised money, but outcry as ‘it doesn’t solve underlying problem’
  • ‘Look with 2 eyes’: Beijing envoy urges South Korean media to report on ‘real China’
  • Coronavirus: new variants raise questions about sustainability of China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China debt: local governments target amateur investors in new round of risky credit expansion
  • Largest Chinese patrol ship in Taiwan Strait opens up diplomatic route to law enforcement: analysts
  • US and Australia must boost Indo-Pacific security cooperation as China looms: deputy prime minister
  • Sri Lanka crisis: political turmoil may have ‘big impact’ on China ties, observers say
  • Businesses confronting new US ban on Xinjiang products say it leaves them confused
  • India to surpass China as most-populous nation in 2023
  • Shinzo Abe: Abenomics not seen as the answer for China’s economic woes with reform favoured over stimulus
  • As BA.5 fuels another surge in cases, new Omicron subvariant Centaurus is also on the march
  • Chinese scientists develop gravity detector with potential use in submarine navigation
  • Shinzo Abe funeral: Taiwanese vice-president in Tokyo but no word from Beijing on its envoy
  • China’s cash crisis culprit named by police, said to have run powerful criminal gang since 2011
  • TikTok owner ByteDance to launch new social media app for young users that will rival Instagram-like service Xiaohongshu
  • China’s foreign minister Wang Yi warns of ‘ferocious storms’ if one-China policy is abandoned by US and Taipei
  • Shinzo Abe’s killing spotlights Unification Church’s ties with Japanese conservatives
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe with funeral procession through heart of Tokyo
  • How Shinzo Abe’s push for reform at home and abroad made Japan stronger and safer
  • Airport staff block Sri Lanka’s president from fleeing crisis-hit country
  • Miss Universe Bhutan blazes LGBT trail as only openly gay celebrity in a land where same-sex activity was long illegal
  • Australia urges US to expand Asia military presence or face ‘catastrophic’ balance of power failure
  • Asia needs leaders of the calibre of Singapore’s Lee Kuan Yew to navigate a messy world
  • US launches Asia charm offensive to counter China’s diplomacy after Blinken-Wang Yi meet
  • South Korea’s Yonsei students under fire for suing underpaid cleaners over ‘noisy’ protests
  • Japan’s energy crisis, Covid-19 woes likely to eclipse LDP’s push to amend constitution
  • Controversial church says mother of suspect who shot Shinzo Abe is a member
  • UK special forces killed Afghan detainees in suspicious circumstances, probe finds
  • Ukraine’s military starts counter-attack in occupied Kherson
  • China’s Ganfeng to pay nearly US$1 billion for its third lithium mine in Argentina to bolster resource for electric vehicle batteries
  • Elon Musk says it’s time for Donald Trump to ‘sail into the sunset’
  • Iran providing Russia ‘weapons-capable’ drones for war in Ukraine, US says
  • Joe Biden seeks to persuade Saudi Arabia to pump more oil as prices soar
  • Italy’s watchdog alleges TikTok breached EU privacy rules, warning the short video platform about targeted ads
  • Non-white patients get less ICU oxygen because of flawed device: study
  • First image from Nasa’s James Webb Space Telescope offers deepest-ever look at cosmos
  • Olympic star Mo Farah reveals he was taken to UK illegally using another child’s name
  • James Bond theme composer Monty Norman dies aged 94
  • New UK prime minister to replace Boris Johnson will be named on September 5
  • Vladimir Putin expands fast track Russian citizenship to all Ukraine
  • McDonald’s Russian successor hit by sanction shortages amid Ukraine war, meaning ‘no fries with that’
  • Nutty idea? Contraceptives for squirrels in UK being mulled by scientists
  • Hundreds of Hong Kong land sites with room for at least 20,000 new flats stuck in limbo as owners call for government action
  • Temasek sees more balanced risk-reward in China markets after recent setbacks of Alibaba, Didi and other tech bets
  • Covid-19 pandemic hits value of Hong Kong’s subsidised Home Ownership Scheme flats, study shows
  • R&F buys up to four years of breathing room as creditors give go-ahead for developer to group 10 offshore bonds into three amortised notes
  • Hong Kong’s listed firms set for more climate risk reporting, with roll out of new consolidated ISSB standards on the horizon
  • Why Hong Kong’s community malls are crucial to the future of the city’s retail sector
  • Chinese hotpot chain Haidilao mulls spinning off overseas restaurants, separate listing in Hong Kong
  • Alibaba, BYD drag Hong Kong stocks to six-week low as Covid-19 cases, tech fines crimp optimism
  • Twitter hits back at Musk, demands US$44 billion deal be completed as it prepares for lawsuit
  • Russian gas cuts to Europe are pushing Germany towards Asian coal, but the demand won’t last
  • Can Hong Kong become an innovation hub for China if closed borders remain the norm? Industry executives see challenges
  • 4 hot new chefs at Shanghai restaurants, from 3-Michelin-star Taian Table to YongFoo Elite and Urban Cafe
  • Sushi without wasabi? The condiment’s future is at risk because of climate change, Japanese farmers fear
  • Chilli Laugh Story movie review: Mirror’s Edan Lui leads Lunar New Year comedy that mixes family antics, naughty jokes and social commentary
  • James Bond movies were filmed there – now it’s being turned into Raffles London luxury hotel
  • Kyoto wants Japan travel to open quicker and foreign tourists to return, though not everyone yearns to see the masses again
  • Change the food court, change the world: eco-innovator Matt Reid on sustainability and his new dining concept in Hong Kong
  • When an American illegally entered China by boat – he did it ‘out of curiosity’
  • Hong Kong’s water supplies built by the British allowed the city to grow and thrive more than any other factor
  • Want to lose weight, but always hungry? Research suggests a way to help obese people feel full while eating less
  • Netflix K-drama Extraordinary Attorney Woo: heartwarming legal drama starring Park Eun-bin really is extraordinary
  • Déjà vu? Kate Middleton and George channel Diana and William at Wimbledon: the young prince’s tennis debut looked almost identical to his dad’s appearance alongside his own mum 31 years earlier
  • 10 richest God of Gamblers cast members – net worths, ranked: Hong Kong icons Andy Lau, Stephen Chow, Donnie Yen, Tony Leung and Chow Yun-fat make millions – but who’s the mystery billionaire on top?
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Elie Saab’s gender-bending autumn/winter 2022-23 show featured men sweeping down the runway in red carpet-ready gowns and capes
  • Big fat Ambani wedding: Mukesh hosted a glam garden extravaganza for niece Isheta Salgaocar at his US$73 million Stoke Park estate, with family including Anant, Isha and Shloka Mehta in attendance
  • Who is Cameron Norrie, Britain’s handsome No 1 tennis star? The leftie who lost to Novak Djokovic was born in South Africa, has an American girlfriend and has earned over US$6.3 million in prize money
  • What do all 3 of Elon Musk’s baby mamas have in common? Meet Shivon Zilis, the Neuralink tech whizz who shares twins with the Tesla CEO, recent girlfriend Grimes and author Justine Wilson
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • West must force private lenders to ease Africa’s crippling debt, say campaigners
  • Post your questions for Zooey Deschanel
  • Russia-Ukraine war live news: dozens killed in Chasiv Yar strike; 80% of Donetsk residents have fled, governor says
  • Shapps pulls out of Tory leadership race and joins Raab in supporting Sunak as Rees-Mogg and Dorries back Truss – live
  • David Squires on … the all-action opening week of Euro 2022
  • The Tory green consensus is breaking – this leadership contest could spell the end of net zero | Helena Horton
  • Heathrow asks airlines to stop selling summer flights as it caps passengers

  • Iran to supply Russia with hundreds of combat drones, US says
  • Philippines mayor orders government workers to smile – or else

  • Shipping’s dirty secret: how ‘scrubbers’ clean the air – while contaminating the sea

  • Bombs, blackmail and wire-taps: how I spent my childhood on the run from the FBI
  • Football transfer rumours: Chelsea to snap up Napoli’s Kalidou Koulibaly?
  • Being poor in the UK is an expensive business | Francisco Garcia
  • Euro nears parity with dollar as pound hits two-year low
  • Uber used Greyball fake app to evade police across Europe, leak reveals
  • Breaking from China’s clean energy dominance ‘imperative’, US and Australia say after new climate tech deal
  • Bill Ackman to wind up Spac and return $4bn to investors
  • ‘Europol ploy’: mass phone scam defrauds Germans of millions of euros
  • Henderson backs Liverpool to avoid Champions League final hangover
  • Nearly $2tn of damage inflicted on other countries by US emissions
  • What Elon’s giving up: Twitter’s best moments – from Boaty McBoatface to Trump’s ban
  • Family and friends of Shinzo Abe attend private funeral in Tokyo
  • The Breakdown | Rugby cannot overlook contact issues and repeated stoppages undermining game
  • US looking at new international body to record rights abuses in Yemen
  • Inside the ‘school’ for men caught paying for sex

  • Bazball isn’t a philosophy or blueprint – it is a response to a game in decay | Jonathan Liew

  • Chasiv Yar death toll rises to 34 in one of Russia’s deadliest attacks so far
  • Maria Ressa: Amal Clooney condemns court after Nobel laureate’s conviction upheld

  • Shireen Abu Aqleh: family of killed journalist demand meeting with Biden
  • Hong Kong to electronically tag Covid patients as it adopts China’s health code system
  • ‘Thank the lord, I have been relieved’: when abortion was safer than childbirth
  • ‘They couldn’t even scream any more. They were just sobbing’: the amateur investors ruined by the crypto crash
  • Cavers find pristine mineshaft frozen in time for 200 years
  • Uber paid academics six-figure sums for research to feed to the media
  • Why the Labour party is praying for the Tories not to vote for Rishi Sunak | Simon Jenkins
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: who are the ‘Moonies’ and why are they in the spotlight?
  • China influenced Kiribati exit from Pacific Islands Forum, MP claims
  • Beachgoers flee sparring sea lions in viral video: ‘Godzilla is chasing them’
  • Was Wimbledon just the start for Nick Kyrgios or will it prove a mirage? | Courtney Walsh
  • First images from Nasa’s James Webb space telescope reveal ancient galaxies
  • ‘What’s going on?’: Wiegman almost lost for words after England trounce Norway
  • England shatter the window of possibility with a giddy delirious win | Jonathan Liew
  • ‘I have many years to come’: Bale targets Euro 2024 after checking in at LAFC
  • Sir Mo Farah reveals he was trafficked into the UK using another child’s name
  • Ash Barty: ‘I didn’t watch this year’s Wimbledon finals. I’ve hit enough tennis balls in my life’
  • Spain will ‘leave our soul out there’ in bid for first win against Germany
  • Thomas talks up ‘phenomenal’ Pogacar as Tour de France’s Covid fears ease
  • Uber’s privileged access to politicians shows the lobby system urgently needs to change | Vicky Cann
  • Patience is vital tactic in Ukraine’s hopes of turning tide against Russia
  • Uber offered shares to media barons for political help, leak reveals
  • The Uber whistleblower: I’m exposing a system that sold people a lie
  • Humans need to value nature as well as profits to survive, UN report finds

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
